-{"id":"gt-ay1r","title":"gt molecule current: Show what agent should be working on","description":"Query what an agent identity is supposed to be working on via breadcrumb trail.

## Command
```bash
gt molecule current <identity>
gt molecule current gastown/furiosa
gt molecule current deacon
```

## Logic
1. Find handoff bead for identity (pinned bead titled "<role> Handoff")
2. Parse attachment field → molecule ID
3. If no attachment → "naked" (no active molecule)
4. If attached → load molecule, find current step:
   - bd ready --parent=<mol-id> → next unblocked step
   - Or first in_progress step

## Output
```
Identity: gastown/furiosa
Handoff:  gt-8v2 (Furiosa Handoff)
Molecule: gt-mol-abc (mol-polecat-work)
Progress: 3/8 steps complete
Current:  gt-mol-abc.4 - verify-tests
```

Or if naked:
```
Identity: gastown/angharad
Handoff:  gt-9x1 (Angharad Handoff)
Molecule: (none attached)
Status:   naked - awaiting work assignment
```

## Use Cases
- Mayor checking what polecats are doing
- Witness verifying polecat progress
- Debug: "why isnt this polecat working?"
- Deacon patrol: track all agent states","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:34:01.430109-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:34:01.430109-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-b1g","title":"MVP Cutover: GGT replaces PGT for batch work","description":"When this is closed, stop using town and start using gt.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. gt spawn assigns issue to polecat and starts session
2. gt spawn --epic spawns workers for all epic children
3. gt session manages tmux lifecycle  
4. gt send / gt inbox work for mail
5. Refinery processes merge queue with semantic merges
6. Integration branches created and landed correctly
7. gt stop --all halts all sessions
8. One successful test batch completed end-to-end

## What Must Work

- Spawn polecat with issue assignment
- Spawn workers for epic children
- Session start/stop/attach
- Mail send/inbox/read
- Refinery merge loop (semantic)
- Integration branch → main landing
- Witness cleanup protocol
- Emergency stop

## What Can Be Deferred

- Doctor checks (use PGT)
- TUI dashboard
- Plugin system
- Federation
- Ephemeral rigs
- Detailed landing reports

## Test Plan

1. Create epic with 2 tasks, spawn 2 workers
2. Verify polecats get assigned and sessions start
3. Simulate task completion
4. Verify Refinery merges to integration
5. Verify landing to main
6. Verify cleanup

## Validation

Run one real batch implementing GGT issues using GGT.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" - just spawn workers for epic, let merge queue coordinate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:09.148751-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:30.970314-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:30.970314-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.196292-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.273483-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.351097-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.431641-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.511124-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.609649-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-rm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.69062-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:32.942855-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:35.053559-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-b2hj","title":"gt orphans: Find lost polecat work","description":"Add a command to find orphaned commits that were never merged to main.

## Problem
Polecat work can get lost when:
- Session killed before merge
- Refinery fails to process
- Network issues during push

## Solution
Add `gt orphans` command that:
1. Uses `git fsck --unreachable` to find dangling commits
2. Filters to recent commits (default: 7 days, configurable)
3. Excludes stash/index entries (WIP on, index on)
4. Shows commit details and suggests recovery

## Usage
```
gt orphans              # Last 7 days
gt orphans --days=14    # Last 2 weeks
gt orphans --recover    # Interactive cherry-pick
```

## Found orphans (Dec 16-20, 2025)
- 3b146c11: Fix mail read auto-ack and add Mayor startup directive
- b6fdc561: Add persistent theme config and fix crew session theming
- 97aba756: Unify gt prime to call bd prime and mail check
- ce769ca5: Add mol-bootstrap molecule for Gas Town installation
- b2219de7: Add bootstrap documentation for new Gas Town installations
- bc82348: feat: Add gt done command (already recovered)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T16:27:25.584819-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:11:11.149526-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:11:11.149526-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt orphans command"}

 {"id":"gt-g44u.5","title":"Spawn --molecule integration","description":"Implement gt spawn --molecule flag for molecule-based polecat workflows.

Usage: gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --molecule mol-gastown-polecat

Behavior:
1. Validate molecule exists and is well-formed
2. Create molecule instance (child beads) under the issue  
3. Find first ready step(s) in the instance
4. Spawn polecat with first ready step as initial work

Implementation:
1. Add --molecule flag to spawn command
2. Call molecule.Instantiate()
3. Query ready steps from instance
4. Pass first ready step to polecat context

Files: internal/cmd/spawn.go

Acceptance:
- --molecule flag works
- Creates proper molecule instance
- Polecat starts on first ready step
- End-to-end test with actual polecat","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:24.519069-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:13:10.086807-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:13:10.086807-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.5","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:24.521029-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.5","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:31.275526-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gby","title":"gt handoff: Unified agent lifecycle command","description":"## Summary

Unified `gt handoff` command for ALL agent types to request lifecycle actions.

## Usage

gt handoff              # Context-aware default
gt handoff --shutdown   # Terminate, cleanup, don't restart
gt handoff --cycle      # Restart with handoff mail
gt handoff --restart    # Fresh restart, no handoff

## Context-Aware Defaults

| Agent Type | Default | Reason |
|------------|---------|--------|
| Polecat | --shutdown | Ephemeral, work is done |
| Witness | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Refinery | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Mayor | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Crew | (sends mail only) | Human-managed |

## What gt handoff Does

1. **Verify safe to stop**
   - Git state clean (no uncommitted changes)
   - Work handed off (PR exists for polecats)

2. **Send handoff mail to self** (for cycle/restart)
   - Captures current state
   - New session will read this

3. **Send lifecycle request to manager**
   - Polecats/Refinery → Witness
   - Witness/Mayor → Daemon
   - Format: mail to <manager> with action type

4. **Set state: requesting_<action>**
   - Lifecycle manager checks this before acting

5. **Wait for termination**
   - Don't self-exit - let manager kill session
   - Ensures clean handoff

## Lifecycle Request Flow

Agent                           Lifecycle Manager
  |                                    |
  | 1. gt handoff --cycle              |
  |    a. Verify git clean             |
  |    b. Send handoff mail to self    |
  |    c. Set requesting_cycle=true    |
  |    d. Send lifecycle request       |
  |------------------------------------→|
  |                                    |
  |                   2. Receive request
  |                   3. Verify state  |
  |                   4. Kill session  |
  |                   5. Start new     |
  |                      (for cycle)   |
  |                                    |
  |         New session reads handoff  |
  |         Resumes work               |

## Who Manages Whom

| Agent | Sends lifecycle request to |
|-------|---------------------------|
| Polecat | <rig>/witness |
| Refinery | <rig>/witness |
| Witness | daemon/ |
| Mayor | daemon/ |

## Implementation

1. Detect current role (polecat, witness, refinery, mayor, crew)
2. Apply context-aware default if no flag specified
3. Run pre-flight checks (git clean, work handed off)
4. Send handoff mail to self (if cycling)
5. Send lifecycle request to appropriate manager
6. Set requesting_<action> in state.json
7. Wait (manager will kill us)

## For Polecats (--shutdown)

Additional cleanup after kill:
- Witness removes worktree
- Witness deletes polecat branch
- Polecat ceases to exist

## Related Issues

- gt-99m: Daemon (handles Mayor/Witness lifecycle)
- gt-7ik: Ephemeral polecats (polecat cleanup)
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:40.806863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:18:22.35369-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.423945-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-eu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.547204-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:24.142182-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gl2","title":"Clarify Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities","description":"Document the cleanup authority model: Witness owns ALL per-worker cleanup, Mayor never involved.

## The Rule

**Witness handles ALL per-worker cleanup. Mayor is never involved.**

## Why This Matters

1. Separation of concerns: Mayor strategic, Witness operational
2. Reduced coordination overhead: No back-and-forth for routine cleanup
3. Faster shutdown: Witness kills workers immediately upon verification
4. Cleaner escalation: Mayor only hears about problems

## What Witness Handles

- Verifying worker git state before kill
- Nudging workers to fix dirty state
- Killing worker sessions
- Updating worker state (sleep/wake)
- Logging verification results

## What Mayor Handles

- Receiving swarm complete notifications
- Deciding whether to start new swarms
- Handling escalations (stuck workers after 3 retries)
- Cross-rig coordination

## Escalation Path

Worker stuck -> Witness nudges (up to 3x) -> Witness escalates to Mayor -> Mayor decides: force kill, reassign, or human

## Anti-Patterns

DO NOT: Mayor asks Witness if worker X is clean
DO: Witness reports swarm complete, all workers verified

DO NOT: Mayor kills worker sessions directly
DO: Mayor tells Witness to abort swarm, Witness handles cleanup

DO NOT: Workers report done to Mayor
DO: Workers report to Witness, Witness aggregates and reports up","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:56.678724-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:12.068964-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gl2","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:05.929877-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-h262","title":"bd ready --blockers: prioritize issues that block other work","description":"bd ready should prioritize issues that are blocking other work.

**From VC**: Blocker-first prioritization in GetReadyWork(). ~100 lines.
Algorithm: baseline-failure first, then discovered:blocker, then by priority.

**Gas Town implementation**: CLI flag or default behavior:
```bash
bd ready --blockers-first  # Or make this default
```

Checks dependency graph. Issues with many dependents surface first.

**Value**: Unblocks parallelism faster. Critical path gets cleared.

**VC lesson**: Without blocker priority, work can starve on discovered issues.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:18.426957-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:18.426957-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-h262","depends_on_id":"gt-zhpa","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:27.664473-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-h28m","title":"Deacon patrol banners: visual feedback on atom transitions","description":"Print large ASCII banners when transitioning between patrol atoms.

## Problem

When the deacon progresses through patrol atoms (steps), there is no visual feedback.
The operator cannot easily see what the deacon is doing without reading the full output.

## Desired Behavior

Print banners on step start and completion:

  INBOX-CHECK - Checking for lifecycle requests, escalations, timers
  INBOX-CHECK COMPLETE - Processed 3 messages, 0 lifecycle requests

## Benefits

1. Scanability: Operator can glance at tmux and see what is happening
2. Progress tracking: Easy to see where in the patrol loop we are
3. Debugging: Clear demarcation between steps for troubleshooting

## Implementation Options

1. In deacon CLAUDE.md: Instruct agent to print banners
2. gt patrol step start/end: Commands that print banners
3. bd mol step hooks: Automatically on step transitions

## Related

- gt-id36: Deacon Kernel
- gt-rana: Patrol System","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:04:14.290474-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:54:55.972256-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:54:55.972256-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in deacon.md.tmpl - startup banner, step banners with emojis, cycle summary banner. Commit 8f47c92."}

 {"id":"gt-rixa","title":"Bug: parseLifecycleRequest always matches 'cycle' due to LIFECYCLE prefix","description":"In lifecycle.go, parseLifecycleRequest checks strings.Contains(title, "cycle") first, but the prefix "LIFECYCLE:" contains the word "cycle". This means ALL lifecycle messages match the cycle action, making restart and shutdown unreachable. Fix: check for restart/shutdown before cycle, or use word boundaries.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T16:17:16.083512-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:20:42.83084-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-rr1i","title":"mol-swarm-cleanup: Post-swarm debris cleanup molecule","description":"After a 20+ worker swarm completed, found significant beads debris:

 {"id":"gt-rt6g","title":"Bug: Deacon session linked to Mayor pane causes heartbeat crosstalk","description":"**Root cause found**: The gt-deacon tmux session was linked to gt-mayor window 2 - they shared the same pane (@283). When the daemon sent heartbeats to the Deacon, they appeared as typed input in the Mayor's window, interrupting user prompts.

**Fix applied**: Killed the broken gt-deacon session. The daemon auto-recreates it with a proper independent pane.

**Prevention**: Investigate how sessions can get linked and add safeguards to session creation code.

Original symptom: Mayor receiving 'HEARTBEAT: Tip: Witnesses monitor polecats...' messages that ate user input.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:00:28.667896-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:08:19.974928-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:08:19.974928-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: killed linked gt-deacon session. Daemon will recreate with independent pane."}
